data.title: European Lawmakers Remain Silent On U.S. Bitcoin Reserve Order Amid Digital Euro Focus
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, European lawmakers have not publicly addressed the United States' recent decision to establish a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve, a significant policy shift that favors early Bitcoin adopters. U.S. President Donald Trump's executive order, issued on March 7, outlines a plan to create a Bitcoin reserve using cryptocurrency seized in criminal cases, rather than purchasing Bitcoin on the open market. This move has sparked curiosity about Europe's stance on integrating Bitcoin into national reserves, as European policymakers have yet to make any major public statements on the matter.

Anastasija Plotnikova, co-founder and CEO of blockchain regulatory firm Fideum, suggests that the silence may indicate a lack of European efforts to establish Bitcoin reserves due to the complex process of adding new assets to national treasuries. She notes that the European Central Bank (ECB) has historically been critical of Bitcoin as a reserve asset, effectively closing the door to its adoption by EU member states. Meanwhile, European lawmakers are focusing on the launch of the digital euro, a central bank digital currency (CBDC), which is expected to roll out in October 2025.

James Wo, founder and CEO of venture capital firm DFG, attributes the European silence on Trump's Bitcoin reserve order to the ECB's firm stance against holding Bitcoin in its reserves. ECB President Christine Lagarde has reiterated this position, emphasizing the EU's focus on the digital euro. However, recent issues with the ECB's Target 2 payment system, which caused significant transaction delays, have raised concerns about the ECB's ability to manage a digital currency.

Despite skepticism, Lagarde is pushing forward with the digital euro's rollout, emphasizing that it will coexist with cash and offer privacy protections to address concerns about government overreach. The European Union's approach contrasts with the United States, where President Trump has taken a firm stance against CBDCs. While CBDCs are praised for their potential to enhance financial inclusion, critics warn of their surveillance capabilities and potential for government overreach. In a related development, Brazil's central bank published the source code for its CBDC pilot in July 2023, revealing surveillance and control mechanisms that allow the central bank to freeze or reduce user funds within CBDC wallets.

data.title: Rex Shares And Osprey Funds Plan To Launch ETF Tracking MOVE Performance
data.description:
According to PANews, investment management firms Rex Shares and Osprey Funds have submitted an application to list an ETF that tracks the price performance of MOVE, a product of Movement Networks. The mainnet of this Ethereum Layer 2 network, built using MoveVM, is expected to launch later on Monday.

The application filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) indicates that the proposed REX-Osprey MOVE ETF will allocate at least 80% of its assets to MOVE or related instruments, through direct holdings and derivatives.

Rex Shares, known for issuing ETFs, has previously collaborated with Osprey Funds to submit applications for listing funds, including products related to meme coins such as TRUMP, BONK, and DOGE.

data.title: Japan Reduces Bitcoin Tax Rate Amid Cryptocurrency Reforms
data.description: According to Foresight News, Japan has reduced the tax rate on Bitcoin earnings from 55% to 20%. This change is part of a broader reform of the country's cryptocurrency regulations.

data.title: Hive Digital Technologies Reports February Bitcoin Mining Output
data.description: According to Odaily, Hive Digital Technologies, a Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin mining company, has released its February update. The report reveals that the company mined 89 BTC during the month, bringing its total Bitcoin holdings to 2,620 BTC.

data.title: BNB Chain Launches $4.4M Permanent Liquidity Support Round 2 for Top Assets
data.description: Key Takeaways:BNB Chain’s $4.4M Permanent Liquidity Support Program enters Round 2 starting March 13, 2025, offering long-term liquidity injections for top-performing BSC projects.Expanded eligibility now includes all asset sectors on BSC, not just memecoins.Daily and weekly competitions will determine winners, with BNB Chain providing non-withdrawable liquidity support to successful projects.Up to $500,000 in liquidity support per project in the weekly competition and up to $150,000 daily for the highest-ranked assets.Winning projects must meet strict security, liquidity, and market cap requirements to ensure sustainability.BNB Chain’s Permanent Liquidity Support Program Expands in Round 2BNB Chain has officially launched the second round of its $4.4 million Permanent Liquidity Support Program, designed to strengthen the liquidity of native BSC assets. The initiative will reward top-performing projects with non-withdrawable BNB liquidity, helping sustain and grow the ecosystem’s most promising assets.Unlike Round 1, which focused primarily on memecoins, Round 2 is open to all sector assets on BSC, ensuring a fair and competitive selection process. Competition Details & EligibilityWho Can Participate?Native BSC assets launched after January 1, 2024.Market cap of at least $50,000.24-hour trading volume of at least $10,000.Excluded Categories:Binance-pegged tokens (ETH, BTCB, SOL, XRP, etc.)Gas fee tokens (BNB)Stablecoins (USDT, BUSD, FDUSD, etc.)Wrapped L1/L2 network tokens (WBNB, WETH, WSOL, etc.)Liquid staking/restaking tokensRound 1 winnersCompetition Structure & Ranking SystemBNB Chain will hold daily and weekly competitions to select winners, ensuring visibility and engagement across various asset categories.Daily Competition:Divided into two market cap zones:Burst Zone (<$20M Market Cap)Mature Zone (≥$20M Market Cap)Ranking Factors:50% Total Trading Volume (24-hour cumulative)45% Market Cap (real-time snapshot)5% Price Increase (24-hour change)Weekly Competition:Open to all qualifying assets, including daily winners.Ranking Factors:50% Total Trading Volume (7-day cumulative)45% Market Cap (real-time snapshot)5% Price Increase (7-day change)Leaderboard Ranking & Reward StructureThe final ranking is determined by a weighted score, calculated as follows:Score = (Market Cap Rank × 45%) + (Price Increase Rank × 5%) + (Total Trading Volume Rank × 50%)Lower scores indicate higher rankings.If two assets have the same score:Higher trading volume wins.If trading volume is equal, the asset with the higher market cap ranks higher.Liquidity Support RewardsDaily Winners:Burst Zone: Up to $50,000 liquidity support per project ($350,000 total across 7 days).Mature Zone: Up to $150,000 liquidity support per project ($1.05M total across 7 days).Weekly Winners:10 winners across 4 tiers:Tier 1 (1 winner): Up to $500,000Tier 2 (2 winners): Up to $400,000 eachTier 3 (3 winners): Up to $300,000 eachTier 4 (4 winners): Up to $200,000 eachWinning Criteria & Security MeasuresTo qualify for rewards, assets must meet these additional requirements:Market Cap of at least $1M.Minimum active holders:2,000 for daily competition.5,000 for weekly competition.Ownership distribution:Top 10 external accounts (EOAs) cannot hold more than 10% of the total supply.Locked/vested holdings are encouraged.Security audit or verified token source code on BscScan.

data.title: Strategy Holds Steady On Bitcoin Holdings Without New Stock Sales
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Strategy, formerly known as MicroStrategy, reported in its latest 8-K filing that no Class A common stock was sold, nor were any additional bitcoins acquired between March 3, 2025, and March 9, 2025, under the company's at-the-market stock issuance plan. As of March 9, 2025, Strategy and its subsidiaries hold approximately 499,096 bitcoins, with a total purchase cost of about $33.1 billion, averaging $66,357 per bitcoin. Earlier reports from BlockBeats indicated that Strategy plans to issue preferred stock to raise $21 billion, potentially to further increase its bitcoin holdings.

data.title: Peter Schiff Announces Personal Bitcoin Reserve Strategy
data.description:
According to Foresight News, economist and cryptocurrency critic Peter Schiff announced on the X platform that he is launching a personal strategic Bitcoin reserve plan, mimicking the U.S. government's approach. Schiff aims to acquire Bitcoin without incurring additional costs by accepting donations, and he has already received 0.055 BTC.

Schiff stated that the donated Bitcoin will not be sold and will be stored in a hardware wallet. He expressed his commitment to holding onto the Bitcoin, saying, "I will live and die with Bitcoin, like a true HODLer."

data.title: Thailand Expands Approved Cryptocurrency List to Include Stablecoins
data.description: According to PANews, the Thai Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) is expanding its list of approved cryptocurrencies to include two major stablecoins, Tether's USDT and Circle's USDC, for trading on digital asset exchanges. Previously, the SEC had only approved BTC, ETH, XRP, XLM, and certain tokens used in Thailand's banking settlement system. This decision follows a public consultation conducted in February, during which most respondents supported the proposal. The new regulations are set to take effect on March 16.

data.title: Ethereum’s Pectra upgrade on Sepolia faces disruptions, unknown attacker mines empty blocks
data.description:
Ethereum’s highly anticipated Pectra upgrade encountered disruptions on the Sepolia testnet following an exploit that led to empty block mining. The upgrade, which was deployed on March 5, ran into issues a few hours later when developers noticed error messages on their geth node. According to a detailed report from Ethereum developer Marius van der […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/ethereums-pectra-upgrade-disruptions-hack/

data.title: PTC Crypto founder Shear joins 1Confirmation
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, 1Confirmation announced that PTC Crypto founder Shear joined as a general partner. 1confirmation founder Nick Tomaino said: "For the past 15 years, cryptocurrency has been about money and infrastructure. The next 15 years will be about useful applications. Brett has long been at the forefront of consumer cryptocurrency, and we are happy to work with him."
